name: Ministry of Emergency Management and Climate Readiness

goals.short_name: British Columbians know their hazards and are ready before disaster strikes

main_services.name: Prepare your household for an emergency

goals.short_name: People, businesses and communities recover from disasters

main_services.name: Get support during and after an evacuation

goals.short_name: Communities and Indigenous partners are resilient to climate and disaster risks

main_services.name: Build local and Indigenous community emergency management capacity
Understand and plan for climate and disaster risk

acts.summary: Assented to November 8, 2023. Repeals and replaces the Emergency Program Act. Governs all four phases of emergency management (mitigation, preparation, response, recovery), the Provincial Emergency Management Organization, local authority powers, agreements with Indigenous governing bodies, declarations of emergency, and disaster financial assistance.

sections.title: Provincial Emergency Management Organization
sections.description: Establishes the provincial emergency management organization within the ministry, led by a deputy minister and provincial administrator, responsible for oversight, leadership and coordination of emergency management across British Columbia (ss. 11-17).

services.name: Lead provincial emergency management coordination and planning

sections.title: Agreements with Indigenous Governing Bodies
sections.description: Sets out coordination agreements, decision-making agreements and statutory power agreements negotiated with Indigenous governing bodies under the Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act (ss. 33 and following).

services.name: Coordinate emergency management agreements with Indigenous governing bodies

sections.title: Mitigation and Preparation Phases
sections.description: Sets out powers and duties of the province, local authorities and Indigenous governing bodies to mitigate hazards and prepare emergency management plans and organizations.

services.name: Create and maintain a local emergency management plan
services.ms_name: Build local and Indigenous community emergency management capacity

services.name: Apply for disaster mitigation and community emergency funding
services.ms_name: Build local and Indigenous community emergency management capacity

services.name: Get emergency management training
services.ms_name: Build local and Indigenous community emergency management capacity

sections.title: Local Authority Response and Recovery Phases
sections.description: Governs declarations of a state of local emergency, local recovery periods, and the response and recovery powers a local authority or First Nation may exercise, including evacuation orders and alerts (ss. 95 and following).

services.name: Learn what to do during an evacuation alert or order

sections.title: Financial Assistance
sections.description: Authorizes disaster financial assistance for individuals, businesses, farms and charitable organizations, and the appeal process for eligibility and amount determinations; detailed eligibility and compensation rules are set out in the Compensation and Disaster Financial Assistance Regulation (B.C. Reg. 124/95).

services.name: Apply for disaster financial assistance as a homeowner or tenant
Apply for disaster financial assistance for your small business or farm
Apply for disaster financial assistance for a charitable or non-profit organization
Appeal a disaster financial assistance decision
Administer disaster financial assistance program operations
